channel.title: Encoders on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink
channel.description: Recent content in Encoders on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink

item.title: PDEC - Position Decoder for SAM Devices
item.description:
Position Decoder - Hall Sensors and Stepper Motor Interface

Overview

The MCHP_PDEC block provides an interface to the Position Decoder (PDEC) peripheral on SAM E5x and E7x microcontrollers. Unlike traditional quadrature encoders, PDEC is optimized for Hall effect sensors (3-phase BLDC motors) and stepper motor position tracking, with specialized features for these applications.

When to use:

  • Hall sensors — Decode 3-phase Hall sensor signals for BLDC motor position (6-step)
  • SAM E5x/E7x only — Dedicated PDEC peripheral on these ARM Cortex-M devices
  • 9-bit angular resolution — Fine position within each Hall sector for smooth FOC
  • Stepper motors — Track step/direction inputs for open-loop stepper position
  • Auto-correction — Built-in phase error correction for Hall sensor glitches

When NOT to use:

item.title: QDEC SAMx - Quadrature Decoder for SAM Devices
item.description:
Quadrature Decoder Using TC Modules - SAM Device Encoder Interface

Overview

The MCHP_QDEC_SAMx block implements quadrature encoder decoding using the Timer/Counter (TC) modules on SAM microcontrollers (E5x, E7x, C2x). Unlike dedicated QEI peripherals, this implementation uses TC modules in quadrature mode to decode encoder signals for position and speed measurement.

When to use:

  • SAM devices — Quadrature decoding on SAM E5x/E7x/C2x using TC peripherals
  • Motor position/speed — Feedback from incremental encoders on ARM-based SAM targets
  • Index pulse support — Position reset or revolution counting using index channel
  • Auto-correction — Built-in phase error correction for noisy encoder signals
  • 32-bit position — Extended position counter with revolution tracking

When NOT to use:

item.title: QEI - Quadrature Encoder Interface
item.description:
Quadrature Encoder Interface - Position and Velocity Measurement

Overview

The MCHP_QEI block provides an interface to the Quadrature Encoder Interface (QEI) peripheral available on dsPIC and PIC32 microcontrollers. This peripheral decodes quadrature encoder signals to measure position, direction, and velocity of rotating shafts, making it ideal for motor control and precision positioning applications.

When to use:

  • dsPIC/PIC32 targets — Hardware QEI peripheral on dsPIC30F/33F/33E/33C/33A and PIC32MK
  • Motor control — Position and velocity feedback for BLDC, PMSM, servo motors
  • Incremental encoders — Decode quadrature signals (X2 or X4 mode) from rotary encoders
  • Index pulse tracking — Absolute reference using index pulse for position reset
  • High-speed counting — Hardware decoder handles high-frequency encoder signals

When NOT to use:

item.title: MCHP QDEC Calibration (Non-Index)
item.description:
Software-based position calibration for QDEC encoders without physical index signal

Block Dialog

Screenshots taken with chip SAME70Q21B - UFBGA144

Overview

The QDEC Calibration (Non-Index) block provides software-based position calibration for quadrature encoder applications that do not have a physical index pulse. This subsystem implements an algorithm to establish an absolute reference position using external reset signals or application logic, enabling accurate position tracking without dedicated index hardware.
